Notice Of Motion For Order To Apportion And Distribute Funds
Off Calendar May-24-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for motion for order to apportion and distribute funds by Plaintiff.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:09:18 a.m. and concluding at 10:44:50 a.m.

Plaintiff Debra Murdock appearing represented by Kimberly Chu, Esq., via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Lamons Gasket Company not appearing.

Craig A. Tristao, Esq. appearing representing Heirs Peter Gregory Avalos and Debra Murdock via Zoom Video Conference.

The following Heirs are present and placed under oath:

1. DEBRA MURDOCK
2. DORIS JEAN FERREIRA
3. DONNA M. CASTILLO
4. PETER GREGORY AVALOS
5. KATHLEEN LAVONNE PETERSON

Counsel for Plaintiff addresses the Court.
Court examines DEBRA MURDOCK, DORIS JEAN FERREIRA, DONNA M. CASTILLO, PETER GREGORY AVALOS, nd KATHLEEN LAVONNE PETERSON.

The matter is submitted.
The Court, having reviewed the moving papers and having considered the evidence, both oral and documentary, and good cause appearing, Plaintiff's Motion for Order to Apportion and Distribute is GRANTED.

The following are entitled to distribution of all net funds obtained by their counsel, Brayton Purcell LLP, derived from all funds (both litigation funds and funds from claims against bankrupt asbestos defendant trusts) negotiated after the December 2, 2019, death of SIDNEY WEBB in the following proportions:

Estate of PETER AVALOS, deceased: 34%

DEBRA MURDOCK
as Wrongful Death Heir: 33%

STEVEN AVALOS
as Wrongful Death Heir: 0%

PETER GREGORY AVALOS
as Wrongful Death Heir: 33%

TOTAL: 100%

The signature of DEBRA MURDOCK shall be sufficient on all settlement documents for the purpose of settling both the estate claim and the wrongful death claims with defendants in the filed asbestos personal-injury-survival and wrongful death action and in those claims made against bankruptcy-related trusts established for asbestos victims.

Proposed order signed as modified.
